pattern: A level-order traversal naturally lands on the bottom row last, so the running sum of the final fully-processed level is exactly the deepest-leaves total.
hints: The deepest leaves are simply all the nodes on the last level you reach.
A breadth-first sweep processes the tree one full level at a time, so the last level it touches holds the deepest nodes.
Each iteration, sum the current level's values and expand to the next level; when there is no next level, the sum you just computed is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: irregular tree, deepest level has two leaves
second example with deeper right subtree
single node is its own deepest leaf
left-only chain, deepest leaf is the last node
perfect tree, deepest level sums all four leaves
deepest level has a single leaf far down one branch

stress.description: large random tree to exercise the level sweep near the node cap
stress.note: BFS level-order wire form with nulls; at least the root is present
